missing_source_description: One or more attached documents referenced in the B01-002 conversation (June 17-23, 2026): likely the I-9 expiry notice from Accenture HR, the user-drafted TPS/CPT letter sent to Immanuel Gladson, and/or TPS extension documentation. These documents are referenced by the user in their statements but their binary content could not be inspected from Stage 3R records.
impact_on_semantic_extraction: Minor. The key semantic facts (I-9 expiry date 07/01/2026, TPS status, user's active navigation steps, HR contact name) are established by direct user statements in the 13 expansion segments. The missing document binaries would at most confirm details already stated by the user. The recurring_responsibility classification and active/unresolved status do not depend on inspecting the actual letter or notice.
mitigation: Full-evidence expansion (13 segments) provided sufficient direct user statements to establish all key semantic fields. The missing source limitation is documented but does not affect the quality of candidate CAND-B01C1-001. Do not reconstruct the letter or notice from context.
notes: Do not infer specific letter content, Accenture HR system details, or TPS extension outcomes from the missing source. Null filename preserved.

missing_source_description: Seven null-filename attachments and/or referenced missing sources in the B01-003 long conversation (164 eligible user segments). Likely: assignment rubrics (Blade Runner paper, Genre Movie Assignment), course reading materials on Film Noir/Sci-Fi/RomCom, and a previous assignment (possibly the original Blade Runner paper before rewrite). The 7 null-filename attachments are likely uploaded assignment files whose binary content cannot be inspected.
impact_on_semantic_extraction: Moderate but not blocking. The assignment type (Blade Runner paper, Genre Movie Assignment), the AI-detection incident (87% Turnitin flag, grade 0, rewrite allowed), and the short film production details are all established from direct user statements in the 70 retrieved segments (first 20 + last 50). The missing sources could contain the graded rubrics, original submission, or rewrite text — none of which are needed for the candidate_type, ownership, or status determination. The 94 unretrieved middle segments are expected to contain additional draft iterations consistent with the established pattern.
mitigation: Full-evidence expansion retrieved 50 segments (last 50 of 164). First 20 segments cover the AI-detection incident; last 50 cover the Genre Movie Assignment. The gap (positions 21-114) is documented and explained. Assignment rubric content not inferred. Null filenames preserved per hr-018.
notes: Seven missing sources assigned IDs b01c1:ms:002 through b01c1:ms:008. Filenames are null; no filenames invented. Likely rubrics and uploaded assignment files but no reconstruction performed.

missing_source_description: Three null-filename attachments in the B01-007 bundle. These are Naomi's Group Log templates provided as formatting examples. The user references them as source material when preparing their own Group Log. Binary content cannot be inspected; extracted text may be available.
impact_on_semantic_extraction: Low. The three null-filename attachments are external source material (Naomi's templates), not user artifacts, per hr-014 (document source-vs-artifact default) and hr-007 (CSCI 430 group assignment: Naomie Bambara = group member/document author). Their inaccessibility does not reduce confidence in the candidate classification (assignment, CSCI 430, group-owned for CAND-B01C1-005 / user-owned for CAND-B01C1-004). Treating these attachments as source material rather than user artifacts is already the correct classification regardless of binary content.
mitigation: All 17 eligible user segments were included in the bundle; no expansion was required. The user statements in the bundle are sufficient to establish all semantic fields. Three missing sources assigned IDs b01c1:ms:009 through b01c1:ms:011. Null filenames preserved.
notes: Per hr-007: Naomie Bambara = group member/contributor/document author. The attachments are her submitted Group Log templates, not user artifacts. Per hr-014: default to source. Per hr-018: null filenames preserved.

impact_on_semantic_extraction: Low. The missing source (likely a resume file) would confirm formatting details already described by the user (single-page classic, BCI Lab leading). The job_application classification, user_owned ownership, and in_progress status do not depend on inspecting the actual resume file.
mitigation: Full-evidence expansion retrieved 30 segments (all 30 omitted from the 50-segment bundle), providing comprehensive coverage of the application preparation session. All key semantic fields are established from direct user statements. Null filename preserved; no filename invented for the referenced resume or fellowship documents.
notes: One missing source assigned ID b01c1:ms:012. Null filename preserved. Do not infer resume content details beyond what is explicitly stated by the user.
